file.markdownDescription:
Path or URL to a per-container compact decisions cap-file

Points the engine at a cap-file whose entries decide whether each container's events are compacted via `encode()` or preserved as `fullText`. Containers not listed fall back to `compactReceiverDefault`.  The container identity is the value of `compactReceiverContainerField` (defaults to the k8s container name), so listed entries are stable across pod replicas.  **File format** (CSV; header row + one comma-separated entry per row):  ``` container,value <container>,<true|false>[:<untilEpochSec>][:<reason>] ```  - `container` — value of `compactReceiverContainerField` for the events   to act on. In k8s this is the k8s container name. - `value` — `true` (compact via `encode()`) or `false` (explicitly   preserve `fullText` for this container, beating the default). - `untilEpochSec` — optional Unix-epoch (seconds) expiry. Past it the   entry becomes a no-op and the container falls back to   `compactReceiverDefault`. - `reason` — optional free-text for audit. Must not contain commas   (would break CSV parsing).  **Example**:  ``` container,value payment-service,true:1735689600:high-volume PAY-101 auth-service,true istio-proxy,false:1735689600:keep verbose during incident PLAT-42 ```  The file is hot-reloaded on in-place writes (the gitops pattern); a Kubernetes `ConfigMap` mount won't reload because the CM swap is a symlink rename, not an in-place write.

retain.markdownDescription:
Retention period for the per-container cap-file before it is considered stale

Defines the retention period (in milliseconds) for the cap-file (`compactReceiverLookupFile`). If the file's last modified time is older than this period, a warning is logged. The cap entries continue to apply; the staleness check is advisory so operators notice when an automated tooling pipeline has stopped updating the file.  **Validation**: must be greater than 60000ms (1 minute). (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: 300000)

containerField.markdownDescription:
Event field whose value scopes the cap-file lookup

Defines the event field whose value is used as the cap-file lookup key. Defaults to the k8s container name (`k8s_container`), so listed entries are stable across pod replicas and a sidecar never inherits the application container's compaction decision.  Set to any extracted field to scope by something else (service, deployment, tenant, etc.). The same value should appear both as the first column of `compactReceiverLookupFile` entries and as a field on every event the receiver sees. (Default: k8s_container)

default.markdownDescription:
Fallback compaction decision when no cap-file entry applies

Controls the fallback decision when no cap-file is loaded, no entry matches an event's container, or a matching entry has expired.  - `false` (default) — preserve `fullText`. Cap-file entries opt   specific containers INTO compaction. - `true` — compact via `encode()`. Cap-file entries opt specific   containers OUT (e.g., audit/compliance containers that must stay   verbose).  Flipping this value is a policy-level decision (affects every event), not a cap-file edit. Changing it requires a pod rollout. The cap-file handles per-container overrides without restart. (Accepts boolean or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)
